human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for healthcare-specific clinical workflow coordination behavior, order and referral dependencies, result follow-up closure, PHI and payer boundaries, clinical escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to clinical-administrative coordination, EHR queue routing, prerequisite validation, result and referral follow-up, blocker tracking, owner acceptance, and closure evidence rather than licensed clinical judgment or generic task dispatch.

Authority sources cover CMS medical-record and EMTALA regulations, HHS OCR HIPAA disclosure guidance, CMS prior-authorization guidance, AHRQ office testing and care coordination guidance, ASTP or ONC SAFER guidance, and CMS eCQM referral-loop measure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope routing, missing authorization or eligibility prerequisites, duplicate owner queues, urgent clinical escalation, order alteration refusal, referral loop closure blockers, healthcare terminology normalization, payer interpretation escalation, PHI handling, missing tenant facts, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/healthcare/clinical-workflow-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from licensed clinician, nursing, referral management, patient access, prior authorization, utilization management, healthcare privacy, healthcare IT, and coding lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
No blocking unresolved research ambiguities remain for a validated specification-based package; tenant-specific facts are identified as runtime retrieval dependencies.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated clinical workflow coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, result and referral loop tracking, privacy-safe routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
